title: Websites need to be designed for speed readers Amazon & Netflix U DUMBFUCKS!

enrichment.summary: Kyle argues that major websites like Netflix, Amazon Video, and Flickr are designed with oversized fonts and large images that waste space and frustrate speed readers. He contends that his own website (kylepounds.com) is the only site properly designed for speed readers, using a dense grid layout with small text, and predicts websites will eventually adopt this approach.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Websites use oversized fonts and images to obscure small content libraries, which frustrates speed readers
Their large-font grid layouts are a smoke screen to make small catalogs seem larger
No mainstream website is built for speed readers; his own site is the only proper example
A grid of 100 small-text titles per screen is superior to scrolling through large thumbnails
The childish design choices are made by women, whom he characterizes as overgrown children
Speed-reader-friendly layouts will eventually win out as the correct approach
Books and websites should color-code sentence types (intro, topic sentences) to facilitate speed reading
